description: GF(2^8) finite-field arithmetic and the Reed-Solomon systematic encoder, modelled on the reedsolo library: gfMul/gfPow/gfInverse/rsGeneratorPoly/rsEncodeMsg. The field is pinned to the UNIQUE GF(2^8) of the primitive polynomial 0x11d via the carry-less-multiply anchor (gfMul a b = clmulModPrimRef a b, a frozen bit-shift/XOR/reduce reference that is the mathematical DEFINITION of field multiplication, not a copyable gfMul), supported by the byte-domain field laws (commutativity, the 1 identity, distributivity over XOR, closure). gfPow is anchored to iterated multiplication and gfInverse to the unique Fermat inverse a^254 (computed by frozen square-and-multiply), with a concrete witness that it inverts. The generator polynomial is pinned to the frozen product prod (x - alpha^i) by its recurrence plus concrete vectors (rsGeneratorPoly 2 = [1,3,2]) and its monic degree-n shape. The load-bearing codeproof obligation is rsEncodeMsg: the codeword is systematic (the message is a verbatim prefix) AND divisible by the generator (the frozen polyMod remainder of the codeword is the all-zero parity block) - the parity is the UNIQUE remainder that drives the word into the code, so the systematic and divisibility halves together pin the encoder, with no self-referential round-trip. Scope: deliberately stops at the field + generator + systematic-encode + divisibility core; the decode layer (syndrome / Berlekamp-Massey / Forney) is EXCLUDED because its natural correctness is a self-referential decode(encode x)=x round-trip that does not guide an implementation in codeproof mode. All arithmetic is discrete over Nat (a byte is a Nat < 256); there is no Float, and no native_decide is used anywhere (field laws are proven structurally / by bounded bit reasoning, never by 256-table enumeration). The byte-domain field laws carry explicit a,b < 256 hypotheses (gfMul reads only the low 8 bits of its first argument and the full value of its second, so commutativity and closure are field facts only on the byte domain).
